title: Roasted Shattering Mediterranean Asparagus Bowl
description: A post-workout, dairy-free-friendly dish that combines roasted white asparagus with a tangy, mustardy tadka.

ingredients.name: mustard seeds
ingredients.notes: use brown or black mustard seeds for a more intense flavor

ingredients.name: coriander seeds
ingredients.notes: freshly toasted will add the most flavor

ingredients.name: garlic
ingredients.notes: minced and toasted for added depth

ingredients.name: lemon juice
ingredients.notes: freshly squeezed for the best flavor

ingredients.name: salt
ingredients.notes: use a flaky sea salt for the best flavor

ingredients.name: black pepper
ingredients.notes: freshly ground for the best flavor

instructions: Line a baking sheet with parchment paper and arrange the asparagus in a single layer.
Drizzle the olive oil over the asparagus and sprinkle with salt and black pepper.
Roast the asparagus in the preheated oven for 12-15 minutes, or until tender and slightly caramelized.
In a small saucepan, heat the mustard seeds and coriander seeds over medium heat, stirring frequently, until fragrant and lightly toasted.
Add the minced garlic to the saucepan and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, until fragrant and lightly toasted.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and stir in the lemon juice.
To assemble the dish, place the roasted asparagus on a serving plate or bowl and spoon the tadka over the top.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley and serve immediately.
